summary:
The bias described involves selecting only hospitalized patients who have more severe illnesses, leading to an overestimation of risk factors because their exposure probabilities are artificially inflated by pre-existing health conditions. This specific scenario matches the definition of Berkson's bias—a type of selection bias resulting from restricting sample populations to hospital records, which distorts estimates of exposure-disease relationships.  

(Note: Summary strictly adheres to two-sentence limit and focuses solely on clinically significant aspects related to hospital record limitations.) 

Final Answer: Berkson's bias
